intro: Literature is the main dish — not a side. Every feast has a centerpiece, and for us it is the text: the reading, the thinking, the conversation it opens. From that center, everything else grows — writing, grammar, philosophy, rhetoric, the habits of mind that serve a student in any field they choose.

pillars.title: Narration First, Always
pillars.body: Before analysis comes comprehension, and before comprehension comes attention. The work begins here.

pillars.title: Writing Grown from the Text
pillars.body: Craft is learned in context, not in a vacuum. Two or three principles per term, drawn directly from the literature.

pillars.title: The Great Conversation
pillars.body: Aristotle, Augustine, Rousseau — not as separate lessons, but as voices already present in the text.

pillars.title: Direction Without Busywork
pillars.body: Enough structure that you never feel lost. Enough freedom to make it yours.

pillars.title: Ideas That Resonate
pillars.body: Love, ambition, justice, beauty — the things that form a person, not just prepare them for a test.

pillars.title: Habits for Any Future
pillars.body: Close reading, careful thinking, articulate expression — habits that serve a student in any field they choose.

pull_quote.text: Finally — something I can use for my high school student. Enough guidance that I'm not lost, enough freedom that it still feels like ours.
pull_quote.label: What We Want Every Mama to Feel
